architecture_fit.note: tanh(20·cos) reproduces a sharp sign-flip at tau=0, but it is a bare scalar nonlinearity, not an attention/transformer delta from base_model.py.

baseline_comparison.note: linear baseline is included but lift_over_baseline=0.0 and worst-slice is tied at 1.0 — the strawman never fails where the method wins.

faithfulness.note: hand-built numpy function with no model, no ablation, and the README does not acknowledge the synthetic nature or propose a causal check.

operating_range.note: only the canonical noiseless unit-norm cosine sweep; no input-scale or noise robustness despite the beta knob existing in the demo.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: mechanism is fully hand-set (no training) and reproduces the target, but it is a trivial pointwise nonlinearity rather than a hand-wired attention circuit.

visual_judgement.note: clean dual-axis sweep plus interactive histogram explorer, but the run dropdown defaults to the empty latest dir so the sweep loads blank until a run is manually picked.

visualisation_rationale.note: README clearly justifies the dual-axis design, threshold marker, and baseline overlay against the three claims it wants checked.

notes: The hand-built tanh(20·cos) head achieves perfect sign accuracy, but so does the trivial linear q·k baseline, giving lift_over_baseline=0.0 and tied worst-slice — exactly the failure the goal warns against. With no model, no ablation, and no demonstrated regime where the mechanism beats the strawman, the central scientific claim is unsupported despite a polished visualisation. Architecture is also far from the base_model.py transformer the framework asks for.
